title: cyrtostemma maritimum
intro: Cyrtostemma maritimum, also known as the Australian sea ash or beach ash, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible fruits. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cyrtostemma maritimum include its potential to treat gastrointestinal issues, fever reduction, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and fruits, which can be consumed as a tea or used in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
